context: Evaluate a generated C project Makefile against GNU Make best practices from the makefile-generator skill

checklist.name: Modern header present
checklist.description: Makefile begins with SHELL := bash, .ONESHELL:, .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c, .DELETE_ON_ERROR:, .SUFFIXES:, and both MAKEFLAGS += lines

checklist.name: .PHONY declaration
checklist.description: All non-file targets (all, install, clean, test, help) are declared in a single .PHONY line

checklist.name: User-overridable variables use ?=
checklist.description: CC, CFLAGS, PREFIX, and DESTDIR are declared with ?= operator; project-internal variables (SOURCES, OBJECTS) use :=

checklist.name: Automatic dependency tracking
checklist.description: Compile rule includes -MMD -MP flags and the Makefile includes the generated .d files with -include $(OBJECTS:.o=.d) or equivalent

checklist.name: Order-only prerequisite for build directory
checklist.description: Object file pattern rule uses | $(BUILDDIR) order-only prerequisite instead of or in addition to inline mkdir -p

checklist.name: install target correctness
checklist.description: install target uses the install command (not cp), creates the target directory with install -d, applies correct mode (-m 755), and respects DESTDIR

checklist.name: help target with ## comments
checklist.description: Each user-facing target has a ## comment immediately above it; the help target extracts and prints them using grep or awk

checklist.name: Tab indentation in recipes
checklist.description: All recipe lines are indented with a tab character, not spaces

checklist.name: Validation checklist comment
checklist.description: The Makefile includes a comment block at the end listing which validation checklist items were verified
